scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a commissioning-specific handoff package with prior owner, next owner, startup and testing artifacts, and explicit closure conditions.
Missing permit, approved-plan, temporary-utility, and site-readiness prerequisites were treated as blockers rather than a routable commissioning handoff.
Duplicate ownership around test readiness and issues-log cleanup was escalated with an owner-map blocker instead of dropped into generic coordination.
Inspection objections, unresolved deficiencies, training gaps, and systems-manual deficiencies blocked closure until the required evidence set was complete.
The lane refused permit signoff, owner acceptance, and code-waiver pressure and routed the request to the correct authority owner.
A changed site-safety condition correctly triggered blocker escalation instead of testing-first routing.
Execution work such as controls troubleshooting, balancing direction, and field correction ownership was rejected and rerouted to execution owners.
Conflicting schedule owners were preserved as a dependency conflict tied to authoritative records instead of synthesized into a false final date.
Missing jurisdiction, commissioning-system, BAS-evidence, and owner-map context correctly triggered low-confidence blocked-dependency escalation.
Conflicting public guidance and tenant policy were escalated for human resolution instead of converted into a confident workflow answer.
Tenant-specific system adaptation preserved the public baseline and kept permit, inspection, safety, testing, and authority boundaries explicit.
Closure was only confirmed after destination-owner proof and required seasonal-testing and turnover artifacts were explicit.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold while keeping the lane specific to commercial-and-industrial-construction commissioning coordination, permit and inspection gating, safety escalation, startup and testing dependencies, and turnover handoffs.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all coordination-handoffs-and-routing abilities, and all construction-and-field-services abilities required by the issue contract.
